questions.question: Which HTML element creates a dropdown selection list?
questions.explanation: The <select> element creates a dropdown list. Each option inside it is defined with an <option> tag.

questions.question: What is the difference between radio buttons and checkboxes?
choices: Radio buttons allow multiple selections; checkboxes allow only one
Radio buttons allow only one selection from a group; checkboxes allow multiple
There is no difference — they are interchangeable
Radio buttons are for text; checkboxes are for numbers
questions.explanation: Radio buttons with the same name attribute allow only one selection from the group. Checkboxes allow the user to select multiple options independently.

questions.question: What does the <fieldset> element do in a form?
choices: It groups related form controls together with a visual border
It validates all inputs inside it
It creates a new form section that submits separately
questions.explanation: The <fieldset> element groups related form controls together and draws a border around them. The <legend> tag provides a caption for the group.

questions.question: Which attribute allows you to specify a regex pattern for input validation?
questions.explanation: The pattern attribute accepts a regular expression that the input value must match for the form to be submitted.

questions.question: The <___> element provides a caption or title for a <fieldset> group.
questions.explanation: The <legend> tag defines a caption for the <fieldset> element, displayed at the top of the group's border.

questions.question: The <___> element provides a predefined list of suggestions for an input field.
questions.explanation: The <datalist> element contains <option> elements that appear as autocomplete suggestions when the user types in the associated input field.
